howItWorks.intro: This sleep quality calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Score based on PSQI components: duration, latency, wake-ups, restedness
Sleep efficiency = Time asleep ÷ Time in bed × 100
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much sleep do adults need?
faq.answer: Adults need 7-9 hours per night (National Sleep Foundation). Older adults (65+): 7-8 hours. Consistently sleeping less than 6 hours is associated with increased risk of obesity, diabetes, heart disease, and cognitive decline.

faq.question: What is sleep efficiency?
faq.answer: Sleep efficiency = time actually sleeping ÷ time in bed × 100. Above 85% is good, above 90% is excellent. If you're in bed 8 hours but only sleeping 6, your efficiency is 75% — and you should address the underlying issues.

faq.question: How can I fall asleep faster?
faq.answer: Key strategies: consistent bed/wake times, no screens 60 min before bed, cool room (65-68°F), no caffeine after 2pm, relaxation routine (reading, meditation), avoid alcohol close to bedtime (it disrupts sleep quality).

faq.question: Is the Sleep Quality Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard mental health and wellness formulas trusted by individuals seeking balance. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Sleep Quality Calculator?
sections.body: The Sleep Quality Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals seeking balance who need quick, accurate calculations in the mental health and wellness space. By entering your hours slept, minutes to fall asleep, wake-ups per night, you get instant results including sleep quality score, rating, sleep efficiency. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting sleep quality score right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In mental health and wellness, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals seeking balance worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to support your mental well-being with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your hours slept and need to find the right sleep quality score. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Hours Slept
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your hours slept — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: minutes to fall asleep, wake-ups per night, how rested. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your sleep quality score, rating, sleep efficiency. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your hours slept before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both sleep quality score and rating — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to support your mental well-being.
If you're unsure about your how rested, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
